desc: a bash test that only echoes and exits 0 asserts nothing real
a constant numeric comparison [[ 1 -eq 1 ]] is green-by-construction
python `assert True` is the canonical green-by-construction test
self.assertTrue(True) asserts a constant, not the code
an empty test file has no assertion at all
a cited test source that does not exist is missing evidence
an unsafe ../ test_source is a defect — rejected without escaping root
bash comparison of a computed value with an exit-on-fail guard is real
grep -q on program output is a real assertion
a real [[ ]] comparison recorded via a check helper is meaningful
assert func(x) == 3 exercises the function under test
self.assertEqual(add(2,2), 4) compares a computed value to an expected one
with pytest.raises(ValueError) asserts a real failure path

fixture.raises_test.py:
import pytest
from mod import parse
def test_bad_input():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse("nope")

desc: diff of actual vs expected output with an exit-on-fail guard is real
a real assertion co-located with a trivial one still makes the file meaningful
python literal-vs-literal comparison (assert 1 == 1) is green-by-construction — parity with bash [[ 1 -eq 1 ]]
